subject: Computer Networking
title: Keeping Software Updated
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Keeping Software Updated.

hook.text: That 'Update Available' notification you keep dismissing? It might be patching the exact flaw an attacker needs.

buildup.text: Software updates fix known vulnerabilities. Attackers reverse-engineer patches to build exploits for unpatched systems.

discovery.text: Equifax's 2017 breach exploited a flaw with a patch available for two months. They just never applied it.

twist.text: Auto-updates help consumers. But enterprises must test patches first — bad updates can break production systems.

climax.text: Patch management tools scan networks, find outdated software, and deploy fixes on schedule. Automation wins.

punchline.text: The best exploit targets a flaw you patched but never deployed.

quiz.question: Why is timely patching critical for security?
options: Attackers reverse-engineer patches to exploit unpatched systems
